turns.content:
Here's the full inventory. I sorted every reference-emitting target into three buckets by *what kind* of edit each needs — because "mention the policy" means something different for a target that already duplicates the rule versus one that's silent.

## Bucket A — Restate their own `ticket.toml` link form (should *defer* to the global switch, not re-specify it)

These are the scattered duplicates — the exact anti-pattern the AGENTS.md redesign just eliminated at the top level, still living copy-pasted across prompts. Each hardcodes `[<short-id> <title>](…/ticket.toml)`, which means every future format change is an N-file manual sync. Convert these to "defer to the Clickable Reference Policy in AGENTS.md."

| Target | Evidence |
|---|---|
| ticket.prompt.md | L35, L42 — full `[<short-id> <title>](…/ticket.toml)` form restated |
| tickets.prompt.md | L36, L43 — same form, plural |
| spec.prompt.md | L27, L35 — same form for related tickets |
| ticket-system.instructions.md | L444, L445, L446 — the most verbose restatement of the whole render rule |
| spec-system.instructions.md | L74, L171 — "link exact ticket folder paths" (no render form, but same domain) |
| spec.agent.md | L43 — "exact related ticket folder paths returned by ticket tools" |

## Bucket B — Reference-heavy output, **zero** formatting guidance (silent offenders)

These emit lists of tickets/specs/code in their response contract but say nothing about link form — exactly the targets you named. They currently rely on the agent *remembering* AGENTS.md. A one-line explicit pointer closes that gap.

| Target | Evidence |
|---|---|
| ticket-next.prompt.md | Response section L32-35 lists "the chosen ticket" — no link form anywhere in file |
| ticket-refinement.agent.md | Whole file creates/reviews/splits tickets; output section has no reference-format rule |
| interview.agent.md | L46 "recommended ticket/spec update", L37 "exact ticket/spec follow-up" — no link form |
| reviews.prompt.md | Reviews many `in-review` tickets + specs + audits + referenced code (L48-53); no render rule |

## Bucket C — Already say "forward-slash markdown links" but don't point at the canonical policy

Half-aligned — they enforce *some* of the normalization rules inline but don't reference the single source, so they'll drift when the policy changes (e.g. if you flip the default mode to `viewer`).

| Target | Evidence |
|---|---|
| handoff.prompt.md | L50, L54, L55 — "markdown links with forward slashes only", entities as links |
| handoff-tickets.prompt.md | L50, L52, L53 — same, ticket-scoped |

## Recommendation

The highest-leverage move mirrors the fix that just landed in AGENTS.md: **don't restate, defer.** For Bucket A, replace each inline `ticket.toml` render spec with a single line — "Render all entity references per the Clickable Reference Policy in AGENTS.md" — plus the one genuinely local rule worth keeping (never synthesize a path; fetch the authoritative folder path from the API first). For Buckets B and C, add that same one-line pointer to each Response/Output section.

That collapses ~15 scattered restatements down to one canonical definition with pointers — the same "single switch" property you built globally, now honored by the leaf targets instead of quietly contradicting it.

Note: this is the recon deliverable — I'm read-only, so I gathered and cited but did not edit. If you want, hand this to the rule/prompt-editing agent and it can execute Bucket A → C in that priority order, since Bucket A is where the real duplication debt lives.

I’m going to apply this inventory directly: first I’ll locate each target file and current wording, then patch Bucket A to replace hardcoded render rules with a canonical pointer, and finally add the same pointer to Buckets B and C where guidance is missing or partial.I’m collecting the exact file paths and current line text for each bucket so the edits are precise and minimal.
I found all 12 target files and will quickly load the two path-scoped instruction files that govern edits in prompts/agents/instructions before patching.

I confirmed the path-scoped rules and next I’m verifying each target’s actual source of truth; if a file is generated, I’ll patch its corresponding .rule body instead of editing generated output directly.
I confirmed most targets are generated; I’m now switching to their canonical .rule bodies so the change is durable and survives regeneration.
I’ve located all source rule entries; next I’m editing those bodies (plus the one non-generated agent file) so a single regen updates every generated target cleanly.
I’ve mapped the exact duplicated text; now I’m loading the remaining rule bodies so I can apply one consistent defer-to-policy line across Buckets B and C too.

I’ve got the exact text edits queued: Bucket A will drop hardcoded ticket-link rendering, and Buckets B/C will get an explicit canonical-policy pointer while preserving the local “never synthesize path” rule. I’m applying those in the .rule sources now.

turns.content:
Implemented exactly as requested: Bucket A now defers to the canonical policy instead of restating the ticket.toml render form, and Buckets B/C now include an explicit pointer to the canonical policy.

What I changed

1. Bucket A (remove duplicated render spec, keep authoritative-path rule)
- Updated [ticket.prompt.md](.agents/prompts/ticket.prompt.md#L36) and [ticket.prompt.md](.agents/prompts/ticket.prompt.md#L42)
- Updated [tickets.prompt.md](.agents/prompts/tickets.prompt.md#L37) and [tickets.prompt.md](.agents/prompts/tickets.prompt.md#L43)
- Updated [spec.prompt.md](.agents/prompts/spec.prompt.md#L27) and [spec.prompt.md](.agents/prompts/spec.prompt.md#L35)
- Updated [ticket-system.instructions.md](.agents/instructions/ticket-system.instructions.md#L446)
- Updated [spec-system.instructions.md](.agents/instructions/spec-system.instructions.md#L74) and [spec-system.instructions.md](.agents/instructions/spec-system.instructions.md#L172)
- Updated [spec.agent.md](.agents/agents/spec.agent.md#L43)

2. Bucket B (silent offenders: add explicit canonical pointer)
- Updated [ticket-next.prompt.md](.agents/prompts/ticket-next.prompt.md#L36)
- Updated [ticket-refinement.agent.md](.agents/agents/ticket-refinement.agent.md#L59)
- Updated [interview.agent.md](.agents/agents/interview.agent.md#L47)
- Updated [reviews.prompt.md](.agents/prompts/reviews.prompt.md#L91)

3. Bucket C (keep local normalization guidance, also add canonical pointer)

- Updated [handoff-tickets.prompt.md](.agents/prompts/handoff-tickets.prompt.md#L51)

Source-of-truth handling

- For generated targets, I edited the canonical rule bodies under [.rule/rules](.rule/rules) and regenerated outputs.
- Global sync command failed in this workspace with a pre-existing path/storage error, so I regenerated the affected targets individually with rule generate-file/generate-target, which succeeded for all edited generated files.

Notes

- I did not touch unrelated session artifacts under .session.
- If you want, I can also run a focused check to identify why full sync-targets currently fails in this workspace so future global regenerations are clean.

# Handoff — Global Clickable-Reference Policy: validation closeout

This session defined one canonical, format-switchable clickable-reference policy and propagated it across every reference-emitting agent target. The policy body, the AGENTS.md render surface, and 12 downstream rule bodies (prompts/agents/instructions) are all in place and byte-verified; the duplicated per-target `ticket.toml` render forms were removed in favor of a single deferral. The remaining work is validation hygiene and ticket closeout — the aggregated `rule sync-targets` gate is RED on a pre-existing, unrelated viewer-api artifact, so the edited targets were regenerated individually. The next agent should diagnose the aggregated gate, fix a known ticket-body typo, and drive ticket 15c39147 through review to done.

## Shorthand And Placeholder Legend

- POLICY-RULE → ce05ee88-e2e0-494c-846a-270aa07c6638, canonical "Clickable Reference Policy" rule body — [.rule/rules/ce05ee88-e2e0-494c-846a-270aa07c6638/body.md](.rule/rules/ce05ee88-e2e0-494c-846a-270aa07c6638/body.md)
- PRECEDENCE-RULE → 7a21f7ef-b76b-4cef-8a7b-7727958088cd, "Instruction Precedence and Exceptions" rule body that defers to POLICY-RULE — [.rule/rules/7a21f7ef-b76b-4cef-8a7b-7727958088cd/body.md](.rule/rules/7a21f7ef-b76b-4cef-8a7b-7727958088cd/body.md)
- SPEC-RP → 954d9807-f357-41e5-9fd4-b1da39e0933d, "Recurring cross-cutting principles" spec — [954d9807 Recurring cross-cutting principles](.spec/specs/954d9807-f357-41e5-9fd4-b1da39e0933d/spec.toml)
- <workspace> → the ticket-api workspace selector, `default`

## Ticket Legend

- T1 → 15c39147-4dac-4bdf-9b4f-b8b51e2a6c6e, "Global clickable-reference policy rendered across agent targets", state `in-review` — [15c39147 Global clickable-reference policy rendered across agent targets](.ticket/tickets/15c39147-4dac-4bdf-9b4f-b8b51e2a6c6e/ticket.toml)

## Key Entities

- [15c39147 Global clickable-reference policy rendered across agent targets](.ticket/tickets/15c39147-4dac-4bdf-9b4f-b8b51e2a6c6e/ticket.toml) — the tracking ticket to close; its description contains the `464a`/`494c` id typo to fix.
- [.rule/rules/ce05ee88-e2e0-494c-846a-270aa07c6638/body.md](.rule/rules/ce05ee88-e2e0-494c-846a-270aa07c6638/body.md) — POLICY-RULE, single source of truth for reference formatting; edit here to change the format for the whole repo.
- [.rule/rules/7a21f7ef-b76b-4cef-8a7b-7727958088cd/body.md](.rule/rules/7a21f7ef-b76b-4cef-8a7b-7727958088cd/body.md) — PRECEDENCE-RULE; its "Formatting conflict policy" block defers to POLICY-RULE (bidirectional cross-link).
- [AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md) — global render surface generated from POLICY-RULE; must stay byte-stable under `generate-target`.
- [rule-targets/10-agents.yaml](./rule-targets/10-agents.yaml) — wires the `agent-rules/clickable-references` node into AGENTS.md.
- [954d9807 Recurring cross-cutting principles](.spec/specs/954d9807-f357-41e5-9fd4-b1da39e0933d/spec.toml) — spec whose decorative clickable-references section was deleted and body reverted; confirm it stays reverted.
- Downstream deferral bodies (12) under [.rule/rules/](./.rule/rules/): 016417ac, 0719f0c1, 084fd4e6, 3fa7e226, 5b9a1792, 62f3b15f, 633ef4f2, 8007eaed, a634822a, b5e0ebf0, c794a4a2, e1f26fb9 — each now renders "per the Clickable Reference Policy in AGENTS.md".

## Findings

- POLICY-RULE source-of-truth was updated (not just generated output); all 12 downstream rule bodies carry the deferral text — verified by grep of [.rule/rules/](./.rule/rules/).
- The old duplicated form `[<short-id> <title>](.../ticket.toml)` now survives only in `history.ndjson` audit logs (append-only, correctly left untouched), not in any live `body.md`.
- Bucket A kept the genuinely-local "never synthesize the folder path; fetch authoritative path from ticket-api" rule and dropped only the duplicated render form — see [.agents/prompts/ticket.prompt.md](./.agents/prompts/ticket.prompt.md) steps 10-12.
- Authoritative POLICY-RULE id is ce05ee88-e2e0-494c-846a-270aa07c6638. The ticket description records it as `...464a...`, which is wrong.

## Decisions

- Single canonical owner is POLICY-RULE; PRECEDENCE-RULE defers rather than restating. One definition, cross-linked both ways.
- Default reference mode is `manifest` (link to entity manifest file). `viewer` and `description` are the alternate modes.
- doc-viewer has no per-entity deep-link route; docs fall back to manifest/description mode by design (stated, not a placeholder).

## Blockers

- Aggregated `rule sync-targets --config rule-targets.yaml --check` is RED on a pre-existing viewer-api submodule spec artifact (reported as `798c9a3c body.md`) that is unrelated to this track and untouched here. Because of this, edited generated targets were regenerated individually via `rule generate-target` / `generate-file`, so there is no single green aggregated proof across all targets.

## Long-Horizon Goal

One switch changes reference formatting everywhere: every agent and prompt renders workspace-entity references identically, defined once in POLICY-RULE, with zero duplicated per-target render specs and a green aggregated regeneration gate.

## Next Actions (execution order)

1. Fix the id typo in the T1 description: change the POLICY-RULE fragment from `ce05ee88-e2e0-464a...` to `ce05ee88-e2e0-494c-846a-270aa07c6638` in [15c39147 Global clickable-reference policy rendered across agent targets](.ticket/tickets/15c39147-4dac-4bdf-9b4f-b8b51e2a6c6e/ticket.toml). First validation: grep the ticket description for `464a` returns no matches.
2. Diagnose why aggregated `rule sync-targets --config rule-targets.yaml --check` fails. Confirm whether `798c9a3c body.md` drift is truly pre-existing (stash this track's edits and re-run; it should stay red on the same file). First validation: the failing path in the sync error is inside the viewer-api submodule, not in this repo's `.rule/` or `.agents/`.
3. Either fix the viewer-api submodule drift in its own store or record it explicitly as an out-of-scope pre-existing defect, then re-run the aggregated gate. First validation: `rule sync-targets --config rule-targets.yaml --check` exits 0, or the residual red is documented as unrelated with a linked follow-up.
4. Re-verify AGENTS.md byte-stability: `rule generate-target --config rule-targets.yaml --target context-engine-agents --check` exits 0.
5. Move T1 through the review gate to done: verify the code-review checklist and spec traceability, then `close 15c39147`. First validation: `ticket get 15c39147` reports state `done`.

## Completion Status

- Action 1 (fix id typo) — not-started — one-line description edit.
- Action 2 (diagnose aggregated gate) — not-started — root-cause the pre-existing red.
- Action 3 (resolve or document viewer-api drift) — blocked — depends on Action 2 outcome; may require a separate submodule ticket.
- Action 4 (AGENTS.md byte-stable re-check) — not-started — expected to already pass; confirm.
- Action 5 (close T1) — not-started — gated on Actions 1-4.

## Definition Of Done

- T1 description carries the correct POLICY-RULE id (no `464a`).
- Aggregated `rule sync-targets --check` is either green, or its residual red is proven unrelated and tracked in its own ticket.
- AGENTS.md `generate-target --check` exits 0.
- POLICY-RULE remains the only reference-format definition; no live `body.md` restates the `ticket.toml` render form.
- T1 is `done`.

## Board / Session Notes

- No board check-in was recorded for this track; check in on 15c39147 before editing if coordinating with other agents.
- Do not touch unrelated memory-api / viewer-api submodule churn — treat it as background activity except where Action 3 explicitly scopes the viewer-api drift.

Execute the next actions in order and continue until all are completed or explicitly blocked.
